provenance: Visual-direction items synthesized from 'Standalone Visual Direction Framework for Scripted Series' (founder ruling 8: checklist source, never a third scorecard). AI-artifact checks are wholly Claude-owned additions - no source document covers AI-generated-video failure modes (see SYNTHESIS_NOTES). All checks are informational pass/warn: they never score, never gate, and never enter any composite. The engine passes their results through untouched.
note: The engine is provenance-blind: AI-artifact checks run on EVERY video, and the app never asks whether content is AI-made.

aiArtifacts.name: Face stability
aiArtifacts.whatToCheck: Faces keep a stable identity across frames and shots: no warping, melting, or character-swap between cuts of the same person.

aiArtifacts.name: Object and wardrobe continuity
aiArtifacts.whatToCheck: Props, costumes, hair, and backgrounds stay physically consistent between adjacent shots: no objects appearing/vanishing or changing form without cause.

aiArtifacts.name: Lip-sync integrity
aiArtifacts.whatToCheck: Lip movement plausibly matches the dialogue audio in language and timing on every on-camera line.

aiArtifacts.name: Anatomy and in-scene text
aiArtifacts.whatToCheck: Hands, limbs, and body geometry stay plausible; any text visible inside the scene (signs, phones, documents) is stable and readable, not garbled glyphs.

visualDirection.name: No story-critical info in the caption band
visualDirection.whatToCheck: No crucial evidence, on-screen text, or decisive action sits where captions or app chrome will cover it (protect roughly the top 10%, bottom 20%, and side 5-6% of frame).
visualDirection.sourceRef: Standalone framework p.11, p.18; house framing standard (mobile-first framework p.4)

visualDirection.name: Reveals carry proof + reaction (+ consequence)
visualDirection.whatToCheck: Every important reveal has a readable proof shot and a reaction shot; a consequence shot is expected on major reveals.

visualDirection.name: Dialogue never masked
visualDirection.whatToCheck: Score, stings, or effects never bury a story-relevant line; dialogue intelligibility stays primary.

visualDirection.name: Eyelines and screen direction consistent
visualDirection.whatToCheck: Eyelines match across cuts and screen direction stays coherent, so spatial relationships remain inferable.

visualDirection.name: Plot-critical objects get readable inserts
visualDirection.whatToCheck: Anything crucial but visually small or text-based (documents, phone screens, jewelry) receives a close readable insert or staging prominence (~85% of frame for text objects).
visualDirection.sourceRef: Standalone framework pp.5, 7; mobile-first framework pp.10-11

visualDirection.name: The hook is an image, not just a line
visualDirection.whatToCheck: The opening hook is represented visually; if the hook is verbal, a counter-image or proof insert carries it.

visualDirection.name: The episode ends on a visual question
visualDirection.whatToCheck: The final frame implies a next action or unresolved threat - a visual question, not merely a spoken one.

visualDirection.name: Critical text/action inside safe areas
visualDirection.whatToCheck: Critical text and action stay inside action-safe/graphics-safe margins (EBU-style 3.5%/5% referenced by the source; localized text still fits after expansion).

promotableMoments.name: Promotable moments
promotableMoments.purpose: Identify which beats would cut into a scroll-stopping ad clip - the industry's #1 promotion decider. Non-scored call-out.
criteria: Peak conflict, reveal, or emotional spike
Works with zero context - a stranger understands it mid-scroll
Visually legible at phone size (faces readable, action clear)
Ends on or contains an open question

output.reason: one line on why this beat would stop a scroll
